input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## Step 1 — Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm the name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes android, ios sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows-target MAUI apps need the Windows App SDK and WinUI tooling, which ship with VS:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, if you only want the build tools (no IDE):
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required workload components:

```powershell
# From an elevated prompt — adjust path if needed
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --quiet --norestart
```

## Step 4 — Set Up Android SDK & Emulator

The MAUI workload bundles an Android SDK manager, but you can also control it explicitly:

```powershell
# Discover where the MAUI workload placed the Android SDK
dotnet build -t:ResolveAndroidTooling -v:n 2>&1 | Select-String "AndroidSdkDirectory"

# --- Or install/manage via Android cmdline tools manually ---
# Download command-line tools
$cmdlineUrl = "https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-win-11076708_latest.zip"
Invoke-WebRequest $cmdlineUrl -OutFile "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip"
Expand-Archive "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip" -DestinationPath "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools"

# Rename to "latest" (required directory layout)
Rename-Item "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools\cmdline-tools" "latest"

# Add to PATH for this session
$env:ANDROID_HOME = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
$env:PATH += ";$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin;$env:ANDROID_HOME\platform-tools"

# Discover available platforms & build-tools dynamically
sdkmanager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-" | Select-Object -First 10
sdkmanager --list | Select-String "build-tools;" | Select-Object -First 10

# Install the latest platform, build-tools, and emulator
# (replace versions with what --list showed)
sdkmanager --install `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Accept all licenses
sdkmanager --licenses   # answer 'y' to each
```

## Step 5 — Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
avdmanager create avd `
  --name "Pixel_MAUI" `
  --package "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel_7"

# Launch it (verify it boots)
emulator -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

## Step 6 — Persist Environment Variables

```powershell
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk", "User")
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("PATH",
  "$env:PATH;$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin;$env:ANDROID_HOME\platform-tools",
  "User")

# Enable HW acceleration for the emulator (requires admin)
# Verify Hyper-V / WHPX is enabled:
Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName *Hyper* |
  Select-Object FeatureName, State
# If disabled:
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All -NoRestart
```

## Step 7 — Scaffold & Build a MAUI App

```powershell
# Create a new project
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run

# Run on Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0 -t:Run
```

## Step 8 — Discover Target Framework Dynamically

If you're unsure which TFMs are available:

```powershell
# List installed Android/Windows TFMs from the project file
Select-Xml -Path MyMauiApp.csproj -XPath "//TargetFrameworks" |
  ForEach-Object { $_.Node.InnerText -split ";" }

# Check available Windows SDK versions on your machine
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include" |
  Select-Object Name
```

---

**Key notes:**
- **Hyper-V** must be enabled for Android emulator hardware acceleration.
- The `net9.0-*` TFMs assume .NET 9; adjust to `net8.0-*` if you installed SDK 8.
- `sdkmanager --list` is your friend for discovering the latest Android API levels and build-tools versions dynamically — never hardcode these.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now and want all the PowerShell commands ready to go. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
